title: Dependency boundaries

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications draft for Dependency boundaries; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: MDN JavaScript Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Dependency boundaries
sources.claim: Module architecture groups cohesive responsibilities, keeps dependency direction clear, and isolates browser, network, and storage boundaries.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Module architecture groups cohesive responsibilities, keeps dependency direction clear, and isolates browser, network, and storage boundaries.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A domain module accepts a storage port instead of importing localStorage throughout business logic. Your goal is to design and implement a module graph without circular feature dependencies or hidden globals. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Control flow and functions; Module loading baseline; Imports, exports and module graph; Composition and inheritance. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Module architecture groups cohesive responsibilities, keeps dependency direction clear, and isolates browser, network, and storage boundaries. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Tiny files can create cycles, barrel exports can hide coupling, and shared mutable singletons undermine tests. This draft therefore rejects: More modules always mean lower coupling regardless of dependency direction.

includes: Separate domain logic, browser adapters and presentation so core behavior is directly testable.
Design and implement a module graph without circular feature dependencies or hidden globals.
excludes: Tiny files can create cycles, barrel exports can hide coupling, and shared mutable singletons undermine tests.

outcomes: Design and implement a module graph without circular feature dependencies or hidden globals.

examples.title: Dependency boundaries: concrete case
examples.situation: A domain module accepts a storage port instead of importing localStorage throughout business logic.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A domain module accepts a storage port instead of importing localStorage throughout business logic.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Choose cohesive module boundaries and enforce dependency direction; file count alone does not reduce coupling.
examples.result: The result follows because Choose cohesive module boundaries and enforce dependency direction; file count alone does not reduce coupling.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications artifact to explain why this belief fails: More modules always mean lower coupling regardless of dependency direction.

analogy.example: A coordinated event desk can model Dependency boundaries, with values, tasks, callbacks, and external messages changing observable state.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Dependency boundaries before using exact platform terms
limitations: Human coordination does not reproduce coercion, lexical scope, microtasks, prototypes, or browser security boundaries.
